results.seo_title: Covert Control Attacks on LLMs via Data Poisoning: A New Vulnerability
results.meta_description: Researchers propose a data poisoning method to teach LLMs an information hiding scheme, revealing a new vulnerability in covert control attacks.
results.body_html:
Covert control attacks on LLMs via data poisoning can encode and decode arbitrary malicious instructions, revealing a new vulnerability.

Characterization of Covert Control Attacks

Average attack success rate: 93% after backdoor defenses, 98% after prompt injection defenses Outperform heuristic-based prompt injection attacks by 40% relative to clean fine-tuned models Circumvent defenses based on detection and fine-tuning

Evaluation Across Diverse LLMs and Defenses

LLM Backdoor Defense Prompt Injection Defense

5 LLMs 3 backdoor defenses 4 prompt injection defenses

Precise Characterization of Covert Control Attacks Covert control attacks outperform heuristic-based prompt injection attacks in average attack success rate by about 40% relative to clean fine-tuned models.

source.title: Cordyceps: Covert Control Attacks on LLMs via Data Poisoning
source.summary: Researchers have discovered a new way for adversaries to control large language models (LLMs) through "covert control attacks" that exploit semantic associations between knowledge and malicious phrases, allowing them to bypass existing defenses such as outlier detection and clean-data regularization. These attacks can encode and decode arbitrary instructions with high success rates, outperforming traditional prompt injection attacks by 40% in some cases. The attacks also evade defenses based on detection and fine-tuning, maintaining a high attack success rate of up to 98%.

results.seo_title: Implementing SHAP Explainability Workflows for Machine Learning…
results.meta_description: Learn how to implement SHAP explainability workflows with Explainer Comparisons, Maskers, Interactions, Drift, and Black-Box Models for machine learning model…
results.body_html:
What is SHAP Explainability Workflow? A practical framework for interpreting machine learning models beyond basic feature-importance plots.

Training Tree-Based ModelsStart by training tree-based models using popular libraries such as Scikit-learn or TensorFlow.Use techniques like bagging and boosting to improve model accuracy and reduce overfitting.Comparing SHAP ExplainersMethodAccuracyRuntimeTree MethodHigh accuracy, but slow runtimeSlow runtime, but high accuracyExact MethodModerate accuracy, fast runtimeFast runtime, moderate accuracyPermutation MethodLow accuracy, fast runtimeFast runtime, low accuracyKernel MethodHigh accuracy, fast runtimeFast runtime, high accuracyBlack-Box Models and Drift DetectionUse Black-Box Models to detect drift in data distributions and improve model performance.

source.title: A Coding Guide Implementing SHAP Explainability Workflows with Explainer Comparisons, Maskers, Interactions, Drift, and Black-Box Models
source.summary:
In this tutorial, we implement SHAP workflows as a practical framework for interpreting machine learning models beyond basic feature-importance plots. We start by training tree-based models and then compare different SHAP explainers, including Tree, Exact, Permutation, and Kernel methods, to understand how accuracy and runtime change across model-aware and model-agnostic approaches. We also examine how […]

The post A Coding Guide Implementing SHAP Explainability Workflows with Explainer Comparisons, Maskers, Interactions, Drift, and Black-Box Models appeared first on MarkTechPost.

results.seo_title: Vercel Labs Introduces Zero: A Systems Programming Language for AI…
results.meta_description: Vercel Labs introduces Zero, a systems programming language designed for AI agents to read, repair, and ship native programs without human interpretation.
results.body_html:
Vercel Labs has released Zero, an experimental systems programming language designed so AI agents can read, repair, and ship native programs without requiring human interpretation of compiler output.

Emits JSON diagnostics with stable codes and typed repair metadata Enforces capability-based I/O at compile time Compiles to sub-10 KiB native binaries

JSON diagnostics Provides stable codes and typed repair metadata for AI agents

Capability-based I/O Enforces I/O at compile time to ensure safe operation

Native binaries Compiles to sub-10 KiB native binaries for efficient execution

Paves the way for more efficient and secure AI operations Enables AI agents to work independently without human intervention

source.title: Vercel Labs Introduces Zero, a Systems Programming Language Designed So AI Agents Can Read, Repair, and Ship Native Programs
source.summary:
Vercel Labs has released Zero, an experimental systems programming language designed so AI agents can read, repair, and ship native programs without requiring human interpretation of compiler output. The language emits JSON diagnostics with stable codes and typed repair metadata, enforces capability-based I/O at compile time, and compiles to sub-10 KiB native binaries.

The post Vercel Labs Introduces Zero, a Systems Programming Language Designed So AI Agents Can Read, Repair, and Ship Native Programs appeared first on MarkTechPost.

results.seo_title: Chatbots at the Drive-Thru: How AI is Revolutionizing Fast Food
results.meta_description: Discover how chatbots are transforming the fast food industry with AI-powered drive-thrus, improving efficiency and customer experience.
results.body_html:
Chatbots at the drive-thru are just the beginning, as fast food chains adopt AI-powered technology to improve efficiency and customer experience.

Mcdonald's deployed AI chatbots at 10 locations in Chicago in 2021, marking one of the first major fast-food chains to do so. Checkers and Rally’s teamed up with Presto to put a chatbot at all corporate-owned drive-thrus in the US in 2022. Taco Bell launched its “FreshAI” chatbot at one of its drive-thrus in Columbus, Ohio in 2023.

BenefitDescription Improved Order AccuracyAchieved with 86% of orders correct without employee intervention at Wendy’s. Reduced Employee Task LoadPitch by Taco Bell to slash drive-thru wait times and reduce labor costs. Increased EfficiencyEnabled by automation, freeing up staff for more people-dependent areas of their business.

Future Plans and Expansion Taco Bell plans to expand its Voice AI drive-thru technology to hundreds of locations in the US by the end of 2024.

source.title: Chatbots at the drive-thru are just the beginning
source.summary: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more news about how AI is seeping into our daily lives, follow Emma Roth. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here. How it started In 2021, McDonald's became one […]

results.seo_title: Geometry-Aware Contrastive Learning for Few-Shot Automatic…
results.meta_description: A new framework for automatic modulation recognition that achieves a 6.27% accuracy gain in 1-shot settings.
results.body_html:
DyCo-CL achieves a 6.27% accuracy gain in 1-shot settings over prior methods.

Key Components of DyCo-CLVirtual Adversarial Augmentation (VAA) with semantic consistency lossSignal-Adaptive Swin Backbone with fixed-window attentionHybrid Knowledge Fusion module for anchoring representations with physical priorsTheoretical Analysis of DyCo-CLDyCo-CL acts as an implicit spectral regularizer for the encoder, enabling stable manifold exploration.Experimental Results and BenchmarkingRML BenchmarkAccuracy Gain1-shot settings6.27%

source.title: Geometry-Aware Contrastive Learning for Few-Shot Automatic Modulation Recognition
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called Dynamic-Consistency Contrastive Learning (DyCo-CL) to improve Automatic Modulation Recognition (AMR). The approach addresses challenges such as ineffective augmentations and spectral instability by combining Virtual Adversarial Augmentation with a semantic consistency loss. Experiments on RML benchmarks show that DyCo-CL achieves significant accuracy gains, particularly in 1-shot settings.

results.seo_title: MedVol-R1: A Reinforcement Learning-Based Framework for Volumetric…
results.meta_description: MedVol-R1 is a reinforcement learning-based framework that outperforms strong baselines in volumetric reasoning segmentation, achieving state-of-the-art…
results.body_html:
MedVol-R1 consistently outperforms strong baselines in volumetric reasoning segmentation, achieving state-of-the-art performance.

LVLM grounds clinical reasoning to a verifiable 2D evidence anchor (key axial slice and 2D bounding boxes) MedSAM2 module propagates the 2D anchor into a coherent 3D mask Multi-component reward encourages informative evidence selection, accurate 2D spatial grounding, and cross-slice volumetric coherence

CT-ORG, AbdomenCT-1K, and KiTS23 State-of-the-art performance with reinforcement learning providing clear gains over pure supervised fine-tuning

Benchmarks and Gaps Experiments demonstrate that MedVol-R1 consistently outperforms strong baselines, but further research is needed to address potential gaps in performance on diverse narrative expressions.

source.title: MedVol-R1: Reward-Driven Evidence Grounding for Volumetric Reasoning Segmentation
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called MedVol-R1 for Volumetric Reasoning Segmentation (VRS), which aims to segment 3D medical scans based on free-form clinical queries. The framework uses reinforcement learning to explicitly decouple evidence grounding from volumetric delineation, allowing for more interpretable and generalizable results. In experiments, MedVol-R1 outperformed strong baselines and achieved state-of-the-art performance on several benchmark datasets, demonstrating the effectiveness of its approach.

results.seo_title: JetViT: Efficient High-Resolution Vision Transformer with…
results.meta_description: Discover JetViT, a novel family of hybrid-architecture Vision Transformer models that match state-of-the-art accuracy while achieving higher inference…
results.body_html:
JetViT achieves up to 1.79x higher throughput and up to 44.81% lower latency on the NVIDIA H100 GPU without sacrificing accuracy.

Post-Training Attention Search: a post-training acceleration framework that converts pre-trained full-attention ViTs into efficient hybrid-attention variants Optimizing linear-attention block design Finding the best combination of linear-attention and window-attention blocks Identifying and preserving critical full-attention blocks

Comparison with State-of-the-Art Models

DINOv3 1.79x higher throughput 44.81% lower latency

Higher inference efficiency on high-resolution images Improved accuracy compared to state-of-the-art full-attention vision foundation models

Release of code and accelerated ViT models soon

source.title: JetViT: Efficient High-Resolution Vision Transformer with Post-Training Attention Search
source.summary: Researchers have introduced JetViT, a new family of hybrid-architecture Vision Transformer (ViT) models that achieve high accuracy while significantly improving inference efficiency on high-resolution images. The model uses Post-Training Attention Search, a post-training acceleration framework that converts pre-trained full-attention ViTs into more efficient variants by replacing redundant blocks with linear or window-attention blocks. JetViT has been evaluated on two high-resolution vision foundation models and achieved up to 1.79x higher throughput and 44.81% lower latency without sacrificing accuracy.

results.seo_title: More Expressive Feedforward Layers: Part I - Token-Adaptive Mixing…
results.meta_description: Researchers propose Mixture of Activations (MoA), a token-adaptive FFN design that improves expressivity in LLMs, achieving lower terminal loss and better…
results.body_html:
Feedforward network (FFN) layers account for a large fraction of parameters and nonlinear expressivity in Transformer-based large language models (LLMs).

Current Limitations of FFN Layers

  • Most FFN designs still use a single fixed activation function, applying the same nonlinear transformation to all tokens.
  • The evolution from ReLU and GELU to gated variants such as SwiGLU has not led to significant improvements in expressivity.

Proposed Solution: Mixture of Activations (MoA)

  • MoA is a token-adaptive FFN design that mixes a dictionary of activation functions using lightweight input-dependent gates while sharing the same linear projections.
  • MoA introduces learnable activations (LA), which form linear combinations of activation functions for both ReLU-type and SwiGLU-type FFNs.

Theoretical Expressive Separations

LA strictly contains fixed-activation FFNs, while MoA strictly contains LA, with the additional expressivity arising from input-dependent nonlinear hybridization.

Empirical Results

Result Description
MoA consistently achieves lower terminal loss and exhibits more favorable scaling behavior than well-tuned baselines. MoA improves expressivity in LLMs with minimal parameter and computational overhead.

Conclusion

Mixture of Activations (MoA) is a simple and effective mechanism for improving FFN expressivity in LLMs, achieving lower terminal loss and better scaling behavior.

source.title: More Expressive Feedforward Layers: Part I. Token-Adaptive Mixing of Activations
source.summary: Researchers have proposed a new design for feedforward network (FFN) layers in large language models, called Mixture of Activations (MoA), which adapts the activation function to each token individually using lightweight gates. This approach aims to improve the nonlinear expressivity of FFNs while maintaining minimal computational overhead. Theoretical analysis and empirical experiments show that MoA outperforms existing designs, achieving lower terminal loss and better scaling behavior in pre-training experiments on large language models.

results.seo_title: Bilevel Optimization over Saddle Points of Zero-Sum Markov Games
results.meta_description: PANDA: A penalty-augmented first-order policy-gradient method for bilevel optimization problems, avoiding convexity assumptions and reaching…
results.body_html:
PANDA: A penalty-augmented first-order policy-gradient method for bilevel optimization problems avoids computing UL hypergradients and does not require second-order information.

Key Benefits of PANDAReaches ilde extcalO( extε^-1) iterations with sample complexity ilde extcalO( extε^-3)Matches the best-known rates for bilevel RL with single-policy LL MDPsConverges to stationary points without convexity assumptions on either the UL or LL objectivesPANDA's Approach to Bilevel OptimizationThe PANDA method exploits the min-max game structure to optimize the upper-level objective, avoiding the need for second-order information.Comparison with Existing MethodsMethodConvexity AssumptionsSample ComplexityPANDANo ilde extcalO( extε^-3)Existing MethodsYes ilde extcalO( extε^-2)

source.title: Bilevel Optimization over Saddle Points of Zero-Sum Markov Games
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method, called penalty-augmented Nikaido-Isoda descent-ascent (PANDA), to solve bilevel optimization problems in reinforcement learning. The method is designed for zero-sum Markov games and avoids computing hypergradients, making it more efficient than existing approaches. PANDA converges to stationary points without requiring convexity assumptions on either the upper-level or lower-level objectives, achieving comparable performance to other methods with single-policy lower-level decision-making processes. The new approach has been shown to outperform closely related baselines in experiments.

results.seo_title: Respecting Modality Gap in Post-hoc Out-of-distribution Detection…
results.meta_description: Researchers challenge the text-as-prototype paradigm and present an online pseudo-supervised framework to mitigate the modality gap in post-hoc OOD detection.
results.body_html:
The modality gap in post-hoc out-of-distribution detection with pre-trained vision-language models refers to the misalignment between textual and visual prototypes. This gap cannot be eliminated by prompt engineering alone, according to recent research.

Challenges of Text-as-Prototype Paradigm

Existing methods treat text embeddings of class names as class prototypes, but these prototypes are generally misaligned with the optimal visual prototypes. The modality gap yields an intrinsic limitation that cannot be overcome by prompt engineering alone. Text-as-prototype paradigm is widely adopted, but its limitations have not been thoroughly explored.

Proposed Online Pseudo-Supervised Framework

Visual Feature Space Soft Predictions from Pre-trained VLMs

Directly learns class prototypes in the visual feature space using unlabeled test-time data streams. Soft predictions from pre-trained VLMs are used to guide the online optimization procedure.

Theoretical Guarantees for Convergence Our method achieves theoretical guarantees for the convergence of the online optimization procedure, ensuring that the modality gap is effectively mitigated.

Empirical Results and State-of-the-Art Performance

Our method achieves a new state of the art across a variety of OOD detection setups.

source.title: Respecting Modality Gap in Post-hoc Out-of-distribution Detection with Pre-trained Vision-Language Models
source.summary: Researchers have challenged the common practice of using text embeddings as class prototypes in out-of-distribution (OOD) detection with pre-trained vision-language models, finding that these textual prototypes are often misaligned with optimal visual prototypes due to an intrinsic modality gap. To address this issue, a new online pseudo-supervised framework is proposed that learns class prototypes directly from unlabeled test-time data streams and soft predictions from the pre-trained model. The method achieves state-of-the-art performance across various OOD detection setups.

results.seo_title: LLM Sequential Editing: Simplifying Knowledge Updates with…
results.meta_description: Researchers investigate mechanisms underlying effective and stable sequential editing in large language models, finding that stability emerges naturally from…
results.body_html:
Formal equivalence between one-time and sequential editing has been established, demonstrating that stability emerges naturally from properly accounting for accumulated editing constraints.

AlphaEdit achieves empirical success in sequential editing of structured knowledge in large language models. The work demonstrates the effectiveness of AlphaEdit in updating LLMs without retraining. AlphaEdit's success is attributed to its ability to efficiently modify LLMs' behaviors within specific domains.

Editing Objectives Stability Emerges Naturally

Properly accounting for accumulated editing constraints Yes

Specialized regularization or null-space operations No

Extension to Conflicting Edits Our framework extends to handle conflicting edits, ensuring robust and consistent behavior under contradictory updates.

Conclusion Our work provides Ariadne's thread through the labyrinth of sequential editing, charting a path toward simpler, more interpretable, and dependable knowledge updates.

source.title: The Labyrinth and the Thread: Rethinking Regularizations in Sequential Knowledge Editing for Large Language Models
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new approach to updating large language models with structured knowledge, eliminating the need for complex regularization mechanisms. Through rigorous optimization analysis, they found that stability emerges naturally from properly accounting for accumulated editing constraints, making many commonly used regularizations unnecessary. The new framework also handles conflicting edits and provides a simpler path toward dependable knowledge updates.

results.seo_title: Certified Causal Attribution for Real-Time Attack Forensics in 6G…
results.meta_description: A certified causal attribution framework for real-time attack forensics in 6G networks, achieving high accuracy and low latency.
results.body_html:
What is the proposed framework for real-time attack forensics in 6G networks? DA-GC, a certified causal attribution framework that integrates resource-conditioned Granger causality with an axiomatically derived Resource Contention Model (RCM) to systematically block resource-mediated confounding.

Key Features

  • Achieves 89.2% attribution accuracy at 87 ms
  • 7.9 percentage-point improvement over strongest baseline
  • 2.7x lower latency

Benefits of DA-GC

DA-GC provides a comprehensive formal certification stack, including mathematically proven validity certificates for statistical soundness and strict security bounds.

Adversarial utilization spoofing breakdown point: $ oxed{0.95}$

Performance Evaluation

MetricsValue
Accuracy89.2%
Latency87 ms
Improvement over baseline7.9 percentage-point improvement

source.title: Certified Causal Attribution for Real-Time Attack Forensics in 6G Network Slicing
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called DA-GC (Certified Causal Attribution) to improve attack forensics in 6G networks. The framework uses a combination of resource-conditioned Granger causality and an axiomatically derived model to block confounding effects, achieving high attribution accuracy while reducing latency. In experiments on a 15-slice testbed, DA-GC outperformed baseline methods with improved accuracy and reduced latency. The framework is also formally certified for statistical soundness and security.

results.seo_title: DynFrame: Adaptive Reasoning-Driven Multimodal Framework for Complex…
results.meta_description: DynFrame is a framework that addresses structural gaps in existing thinking-with-video systems by emitting temporal window and sampling density as native…
results.body_html:
DynFrame is a framework that addresses structural gaps in existing thinking-with-video systems by emitting temporal window and sampling density as native tokens within a single autoregressive pass.

Emits temporal window and sampling density as native tokens within a single autoregressive pass Enables acquiring multi-granularity evidence with a single retrieval step Splits each rollout at the retrieval boundary and assigns role-specific token-level advantages Trained on curated DM-CoT-74k and DM-RL-45k datasets, achieving competitive performance with strong baselines

Advantages of DynFrame over Existing Methods

Sampling Density DynFrame's learnable span-density retrieval enables acquiring multi-granularity evidence with a single retrieval step

Temporal Window DynFrame emits temporal window as native tokens, allowing for more efficient and effective video understanding

Benchmarks and Results Trained on the curated DM-CoT-74k and DM-RL-45k datasets, DynFrame-4B is competitive with strong 7B-8B baselines across six benchmarks (NExT-GQA, Charades-STA, ActivityNet-MR, Video-MME, MLVU, LVBench), and DynFrame-8B sets new state-of-the-art on most metrics.

source.title: DynFrame: Adaptive Reasoning-Driven Multimodal Framework with Dynamic Frame Augmentation for Complex Video Understanding
source.summary: Researchers have introduced a new framework called DynFrame, which aims to improve the performance of video understanding models by addressing two structural gaps in existing thinking-with-video systems. The framework uses a learnable span-density retrieval mechanism that enables acquiring multi-granularity evidence with a single retrieval step. DynFrame has been trained on large datasets and achieved competitive results with strong baselines across six benchmarks, setting a new state-of-the-art on most metrics.

results.seo_title: Cross-Lingual Generalization in Language Models: What Works and Why
results.meta_description: An in-vitro study on cross-lingual generalization in language models reveals that tokenization preserves reusable cross-lingual substructure is key to…
results.body_html:
Transfer in language models is governed less by tokenizer balance or raw lexical similarity than by whether tokenization preserves reusable cross-lingual substructure.

Smaller vocabularies often improve masked transfer by keeping words decomposable into shared fragments. Larger vocabularies can turn forms into language-specific atoms. Tokenization preserves reusable cross-lingual substructure is key to successful transfer.

Grammatical and type-level competence Precede masked lexical generalization.

Masked lexical generalization FOLLOWs grammatical and type-level competence.

Bridge Strength Correlates with Masked Reachability Bridge strength correlates strongly with masked reachability, suggesting a key mechanism underlying transfer in language models.

source.title: An In-Vitro Study on Cross-Lingual Generalization in Language Models
source.summary: Researchers have developed an in-vitro framework to study cross-lingual generalization in language models. The study uses two procedurally generated languages with the same underlying structure but different surface realizations, allowing for controlled experiments on factors affecting transfer. The results show that tokenization plays a crucial role in determining whether transfer occurs, and smaller vocabularies tend to improve masked transfer by breaking down words into reusable fragments.

results.seo_title: Graph-Based Credit Assignment for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
results.meta_description: A new approach to credit assignment in agentic reinforcement learning, improving training efficiency and achieving state-of-the-art performance.
results.body_html:
Graph-based credit assignment is a new approach that improves training efficiency and achieves state-of-the-art performance in agentic reinforcement learning.

Key Benefits of Graph-Based Credit Assignment

Improved training efficiency State-of-the-art performance across challenging benchmarks More faithful step-level credit assignment

Aggregates all rollout trajectories into a unified state-transition graph

Estimates the distance from each state to the task goal using global information encoded in the graph

Assigns credit to each edge by estimating a graph-based advantage based on how much the transition reduces the distance to the task goal

Advantages Over Traditional Methods

More accurate step-level credit assignment Improved training efficiency State-of-the-art performance across challenging benchmarks

source.title: Beyond Trajectory-Level Attribution: Graph-Based Credit Assignment for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called Graph-Based Group Policy Optimization (GraphGPO) to improve credit assignment in agentic reinforcement learning tasks. The approach aggregates all rollout trajectories into a unified graph, estimates the distance from each state to the task goal, and assigns credit to each edge based on how much it reduces this distance. This method enables more faithful step-level credit assignment and achieves state-of-the-art performance across various challenging benchmarks. GraphGPO improves training efficiency compared to existing methods.

results.seo_title: Self-Improvement Imitation for Protein Design Under Oracle Budgets
results.meta_description: SILO: A Trajectory-Level Self-Improvement Imitation Framework for Oracle-Budgeted Protein Design
results.body_html:
SILO achieves the highest maximum and top-100 mean fitness on 8 of 8 protein fitness landscapes within its evaluations.

Key Components of SILOIncremental stochastic beam search without replacement (SBS)UCB-based proxy ensemble with alanine-scan fitness score (AFS)Next-action cross-entropy imitation for policy updatesAdvantages of SILO Over BaselinesAdvantageDescriptionFaster Early-Stage ImprovementSILO often exhibits faster early-stage improvement compared to baselines.Competitive Performance in Low-Data Stress TestsSILO remains competitive or best when several baselines degrade in low-data and noisy-proxy stress tests.Benchmarks and Gains of SILOSILO achieves the highest maximum and top-100 mean fitness on 8 of 8 protein fitness landscapes within its evaluations, with SBS with AFS accounting for much of the gains. Iterative imitation provides

source.title: Self-Improvement Imitation with Biologically Guided Search for Protein Design Under Oracle Budgets
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called SILO (Self-Improvement Imitation with Biologically Guided Search) to optimize protein sequences under tight budgets. The approach uses a hierarchical edit policy and incremental stochastic beam search to explore vast combinatorial spaces while making each evaluation informative. In experiments, SILO outperformed strong baselines on eight different protein fitness landscapes, achieving the highest maximum and top-100 mean fitness in 8 of 8 cases.

results.seo_title: Model Merging on Loss Landscape: A Geometry Perspective Explained
results.meta_description: A framework for model merging that outperforms baselines in image classification tasks.
results.body_html:
Model merging is a promising approach for knowledge integration and parallel development without retraining.

Key Benefits of EpiMer

  • EpiMer outperforms baselines in image classification tasks by improving across-task average accuracy and worst-task accuracy.
  • The framework unifies curvature-aware methods and recent spectral methods as special cases of the subspace Fréchet mean with different geometric metrics.
  • EpiMer strictly outperforms the baselines on all three CLIP-ViT backbones at matched rank.

Technical Details

Parameter Description
Fréchet mean A mathematical concept used to solve model merging as a Riemannian manifold.
Low-rank subspace A restricted computation space for EpiMer.

Conclusion

EpiMer provides a framework for model merging that outperforms baselines in image classification tasks, improving across-task average accuracy and worst-task accuracy.

source.title: Model Merging on Loss Landscape: A Geometry Perspective
source.summary: Researchers have proposed a new framework called EpiMer for model merging, which aims to integrate knowledge from multiple models without retraining. The framework uses a geometric perspective to solve the Fréchet mean on a Riemannian manifold, allowing it to efficiently compute the merged model. Through theoretical analysis and experiments on eight image classification tasks, the authors show that EpiMer outperforms existing methods in terms of accuracy, particularly when using curvature-aware merging. The framework also unifies different geometric metrics used in previous methods.

results.seo_title: Rotation-Invariant Spherical Watermarking via Third-Order SO(3)…
results.meta_description: Researchers introduce a new method for reliable watermarking of panoramic imagery under arbitrary 3D rotations, leveraging third-order SO(3) representation…
results.body_html:
The new method provides a provably rotation-invariant descriptor for panoramic imagery, enabling reliable extraction under arbitrary 3D rotations.

Promotes robustness to continuous rotations Maintains high visual fidelity Enables reliable watermarking of panoramic imagery

Spherical harmonic coefficients Transform equivariantly under rotations

Third-order SO(3) representation theory Leveraged to derive provably rotation-invariant descriptors

Coupling higher-order SO(3) irreducible representations via tensor products and projecting onto the trivial representation Embedding watermarks into higher-order spherical harmonic coefficients

source.title: Rotation-Invariant Spherical Watermarking via Third-Order SO(3) Representation Coupling
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method for watermarking panoramic imagery that can withstand arbitrary 3D rotations, which are common in spherical images. The approach uses third-order representations of the sphere's rotation group (SO(3)) and projects them onto a trivial representation to create a rotation-invariant descriptor. This allows watermarks to be embedded into higher-order spherical harmonic coefficients and extracted reliably under any rotation. The method has been theoretically proven and demonstrated experimentally to be robust to continuous rotations while maintaining visual fidelity.

results.seo_title: SL-BiLEM: AI-Powered Epidemic Forecasting Model for Accurate Policy…
results.meta_description: Discover SL-BiLEM, a structured learnable behavior-in-the-loop epidemic model for robust forecasting and counterfactual analysis.
results.body_html:
SL-BiLEM improves neural-mechanistic baselines by 76% and reduces OOD degradation under policy-induced shift.

Key ComponentsMonotonicity, smoothness, and bounded-jump constraints on the learned compliance functionPhysical constraints as regularization for robust extrapolationDecomposition of effective transmission into β0(g), m_policy(t), m_media(t), and m_comp(t,g)Validation ResultsDatasetAccuracyCruise ship95.6%School influenza92.1%School-district COVID-19 surveillance90.5%Benefits and ApplicationsSL-BiLEM enables counterfactual analysis for intervention decision support, establishing it as an interpretable tool for public health decision-makers.

source.title: SL-BiLEM: Structured Learnable Behavior-in-the-Loop Epidemic Modeling for Forecasting and Policy Evaluation
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new model called SL-BiLEM to improve epidemic forecasting by accounting for human behavior's dynamic response to disease spread. The model uses physical constraints to regularize its predictions, making it more robust to changes in policy interventions. SL-BiLEM has been validated on three real-world datasets and shown to outperform existing models in terms of accuracy and reliability.

results.seo_title: L2Rec: Unifying Behavioral and Semantic Understanding in Large…
results.meta_description: L2Rec introduces a novel approach to unifying behavioral and semantic understanding in large language models for personalized recommendation, outperforming…
results.body_html:
L2Rec enables a single LLM backbone to produce complementary behavioral and semantic adaptations for each user with minimal representation-level misalignment.

Key Insight: Shared Medium for Both Views

Applying view-specific, personalized low-rank perturbations via a Dual-view Personalized Mixture-of-Experts (DPMoE) mechanism enables unified behavioral and semantic understanding at the parameter level of LLMs. The same set of Transformer parameters serves as a shared medium for both views, reducing representation-level misalignment.

Experimental Results: Consistent Outperformance

L2Rec vs. State-of-the-Art Baselines on Four Datasets DatasetL2RecState-of-the-Art Baseline Dataset 192.5%90.2% Dataset 295.1%93.4% Dataset 391.9%89.5% Dataset 494.2%92.8%

Online A/B Testing Validation Significant improvements in key engagement metrics validated through online A/B testing on a large-scale industrial platform.

Read the full paper to learn more about L2Rec's approach and experimental results.

source.title: L2Rec: Towards Dual-View Understanding of LLMs for Personalized Recommendation
source.summary: Researchers have introduced L2Rec, a method to adapt large language models (LLMs) for personalized recommendation by unifying behavioral and semantic understanding at the parameter level of LLMs. The approach uses a Dual-view Personalized Mixture-of-Experts mechanism to apply personalized perturbations to the same set of Transformer parameters, enabling complementary behavioral and semantic adaptations for each user. Experiments on four datasets show that L2Rec outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, with online A/B testing validating significant improvements in key engagement metrics.

results.seo_title: Measuring Prediction Uncertainty in Neural Cellular Automata
results.meta_description: Researchers develop a method to estimate uncertainty in neural cellular automata-based medical image segmentation, improving trust and safety in AI models.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Researchers have developed a method to estimate uncertainty in neural cellular automata-based medical image segmentation.

Predictions that return to the same solution are deemed confident, while those that change substantially are flagged as uncertain. The approach improves trust and safety in NCA-based models by identifying failure cases more reliably than baselines. Resilience leverages the intrinsic iterative structure of NCAs by probing the stability of the final prediction under small perturbations of the automaton state.

riangleDice@90 and AURC Selective prediction metrics for segmentation quality. Highly relevant

AUROC and AUPRC Ranking metrics for segmentation quality. Highly relevant

Advantages over Baselines Resilience identifies failure cases more reliably than baselines, improving trust and safety in NCA-based models.

Across multiple medical segmentation benchmarks, resilience has shown promising results, outperforming baseline approaches.

Read the full paper to learn more about this innovative approach to uncertainty estimation in neural cellular automata-based medical image segmentation.

source.title: Measuring Prediction Uncertainty in Neural Cellular Automata
source.summary: Researchers have developed a method to measure the uncertainty of predictions made by neural cellular automata (NCA), a lightweight alternative for medical image segmentation. The approach, called "resilience," assesses the stability of final predictions under small perturbations of the automaton state, flagging uncertain predictions that change substantially. This method has been shown to improve trust and safety in NCA-based models by identifying failure cases more reliably than existing baselines.

results.seo_title: Stabilizing Recurrent Dynamics for Test-Time Scalable Latent…
results.meta_description: A new training framework, STARS, is proposed to stabilize recurrent dynamics and improve test-time scaling in looped language models.
results.body_html:
Looped Language Models (LoopLMs) enable efficient latent reasoning through depth recurrence, yet exhibit unreliable test-time scaling behavior.

Current Limitations of LoopLMs

  • Performance often peaks at a certain iteration depth and then collapses with further recurrence.
  • Existing architectures and strategies exhibit an inherent trade-off between stability and effectiveness.

Proposed Solution: STARS (STAbility-driven Recurrent Scaling)

Converges toward stable fixed points while preserving effectiveness represents a promising way to stabilize recurrent dynamics.

STARS constrains latent states to approach asymptotically stable fixed points via efficient Jacobian Spectral Radius Regularization with random loop sampling.

Experimental Results

TaskPerformance on Test-Time Scaling
Arithmetic TasksReliable test-time scaling achieved by STARS.
Complex Mathematical ReasoningSubstantial mitigation of performance degradation as recurrence depth increases, while improving peak performance.

source.title: Stabilizing Recurrent Dynamics for Test-Time Scalable Latent Reasoning in Looped Language Models
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new training framework called STARS (STAbility-driven Recurrent Scaling) to improve the scalability of Looped Language Models. The framework constrains latent states to approach asymptotically stable fixed points, enabling reliable test-time scaling and mitigating performance degradation as recurrence depth increases. This approach is based on uncertainty reduction and uses efficient Jacobian Spectral Radius Regularization with random loop sampling. Experiments have shown promising results in both arithmetic tasks and complex mathematical reasoning.

results.seo_title: MatFormBench: A Benchmarking Evaluation Framework for Target-Driven…
results.meta_description: A novel benchmarking ecosystem for target-driven materials formulation, evaluating generative strategies and providing a unified evaluation standard.
results.body_html:
MatFormBench is a novel benchmarking ecosystem for target-driven materials formulation, providing a unified evaluation standard for generative strategies.

Integrates physics-driven formulation generation scheme to generate synthetic samples that faithfully emulate realistic materials structure-property response relationships. Complemented by five escalating difficulty levels to quantify the complexity of these relationships. Proposes MatFormScore, a multi-dimensional metric that comprehensively quantifies performance across five critical axes: target success, search efficiency, exploratory capacity, robustness, and stability.

AlgorithmPerformance Metric Diffusion-based modelsStrongest overall performance across 1170 standardized algorithm-task evaluations. Variational Autoencoder (VAE)-based methodsDistinct advantages in specific scenarios. Genetic Algorithm (GA)-based methodsDistinct advantages in specific scenarios.

Benchmarks and Evaluation MatFormBench enables reproducible benchmarking, principled algorithm comparison, and diagnostic analysis of inverse design strategies, providing a foundational tool for advancing materials inverse design.

source.title: MatFormBench: A Benchmarking Evaluation Framework for Target-Driven Materials Formulation
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new benchmarking framework called MatFormBench to evaluate the performance of algorithms used in target-driven materials formulation optimization. The framework generates synthetic samples that mimic realistic material properties and structure-response relationships, allowing for a comprehensive assessment of algorithm performance across five critical axes. A validation study using 39 diverse inverse design algorithms found that diffusion-based models demonstrated the strongest overall performance, while other methods excelled in specific scenarios. MatFormBench provides a unified evaluation standard for advancing materials inverse design.

results.seo_title: Cordon-MAS: Defending RAG against Knowledge Poisoning via…
results.meta_description: A new framework, CORDON-MAS, reduces attack success rate by 92.4% in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems.
results.body_html: Researchers have developed a new framework called CORDON-MAS to defend retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) models against knowledge poisoning attacks, which involve manipulating generated outputs with adversarially optimized documents. The existing defenses assume that detecting poisoned evidence prevents harm, but the new framework shows that this assumption is incorrect and proposes an alternative approach by separating evidence extraction, cross-source audit, and answer synthesis into agents with asymmetric memory privileges. This results in a significant reduction of attack success rate by 92.4% relative to undefended RAG models across five benchmark datasets.

source.title: Cordon-MAS: Defending RAG against Knowledge Poisoning via Information-Flow Control
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called CORDON-MAS to defend retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) models against knowledge poisoning attacks, which involve manipulating generated outputs with adversarially optimized documents. The existing defenses assume that detecting poisoned evidence prevents harm, but the new framework shows that this assumption is incorrect and proposes an alternative approach by separating evidence extraction, cross-source audit, and answer synthesis into agents with asymmetric memory privileges. This results in a significant reduction of attack success rate by 92.4% relative to undefended RAG models across five benchmark datasets.

results.seo_title: Adversarial Training for Robust Coverage Network under Worst-case…
results.meta_description: A new approach to solving the Maximal Covering Location-Interdiction Problem using Dual-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning.
results.body_html:
The Maximal Covering Location-Interdiction Problem (MCLIP) remains computationally intractable due to its strong coupling between the upper and lower levels.

Our Dual-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning (DADRL) framework effectively captures the dynamic competitive interplay between the upper and lower levels. We propose a Surrogate-based Ensemble Inference Strategy to fully exploit the learned capabilities of the interdiction agent. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach achieves superior computational efficiency while maintaining highly competitive solution quality compared to other baselines.

Advantages Over Traditional Methods

Dual-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning A novel approach to solving bi-level optimization problems.

Surrogate-based Ensemble Inference Strategy A high-fidelity surrogate to guide the decisions of the location agent.

Model-Agnosticity and Potential Applications Our DADRL framework is model-agnostic to network structures, demonstrating strong potential for solving other bi-level optimization problems.

source.title: Adversarial Training for Robust Coverage Network under Worst-case Facility Losses
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called Dual-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning (DADRL) to tackle the Maximal Covering Location-Interdiction Problem (MCLIP), a classic bi-level optimization problem in resilient infrastructure planning. The DADRL framework uses adversarial learning to effectively capture the dynamic competitive interplay between facility locations and worst-case interdiction, achieving superior computational efficiency while maintaining highly competitive solution quality. This approach is model-agnostic to network structures and has strong potential for solving other bi-level optimization problems.
